task_completion.description: Create 'Museums' class extending 'Attractions' with specified properties
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing OrientDB Studio Schema page or console commands (e.g., CREATE CLASS Museums EXTENDS Attractions) and the creation of properties: Name, Collection, FreeEntry, AnnualVisitors, City, and Country.

task_completion.description: Insert the 5 specified museum records into the 'Museums' class
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing INSERT commands or a database browse view displaying the 5 museums (Louvre Museum, British Museum, Metropolitan Museum of Art, Uffizi Gallery, Rijksmuseum) with their respective data in the Museums class.

task_completion.description: Add 'Category' property to the parent 'Attractions' class
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Schema page for the 'Attractions' class with a new 'Category' property of type STRING, or the corresponding CREATE PROPERTY command.

task_completion.description: Update all records in the 'Museums' class to set Category to 'museum'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing an UPDATE command (e.g., UPDATE Museums SET Category = 'museum') or a data view where the Category field for the museum records is populated with 'museum'.

task_completion.description: Execute the polymorphic query on the Attractions class
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the execution of the exact query 'SELECT Name, Category, @class FROM Attractions' in OrientDB Studio or console, returning results that include the museum records with their @class shown as 'Museums'.
Screenshots showing a terminal window or text editor displaying the contents of /home/ga/attraction_hierarchy_report.txt containing the results of the polymorphic query.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OrientDB database (via Studio GUI or console) to perform the schema changes and queries.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of OrientDB Studio web interface or OrientDB console in the screenshots, rather than just a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the polymorphic query on the parent class rather than faking it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The query executed must be 'SELECT Name, Category, @class FROM Attractions' and not a direct query on the 'Museums' class.

integrity.description: The exported report file was generated from actual query results, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of the report file should match the database output format, and there should be evidence of the query being run before the file was created.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a 'Museums' class that extends 'Attractions', inserting 5 specific real-world museums (Louvre, British Museum, Metropolitan Museum of Art, Uffizi Gallery, Rijksmuseum), adding a 'Category' property to 'Attractions', updating the museums to have Category='museum', and exporting the results of 'SELECT Name, Category, @class FROM Attractions' to /home/ga/attraction_hierarchy_report.txt. The British Museum and Rijksmuseum visitor counts (5.8M and 2.7M) are verified 2023 statistics.
